component.name: Autowire
component.summary: Automatically route audio when devices connect

description.p: Autowire is a lightweight, set-and-forget audio profile manager for GNOME. Define a profile once — link a hardware device (USB dock, Bluetooth headset, HDMI monitor) to a set of default audio inputs and outputs — and Autowire silently applies it every time that device connects.

No more manually switching four dropdowns in Settings every time you move between your desk and your laptop. Autowire runs as a background service and reacts to PipeWire/WirePlumber hardware events instantly, before you even play a sound.

ul.li: Create named Audio Profiles linked to any USB, Bluetooth, or HDMI device
Automatically switch default output (speakers, headphones) and input (microphone)
Event-driven daemon — near-zero resource usage at idle
Clean Libadwaita interface that matches GNOME Settings
Profiles stored locally in a simple JSON file
Auto-switch for calls — daemon detects active mic streams and switches between HSP/HFP and A2DP automatically

screenshot.caption: Profile list with a configured device

description.p: Stream migration fix, pactl fallback, DaemonEngine class, Flatpak polish, and expanded test suite.

ul.li: DaemonEngine class — all mutable state encapsulated in instance; module-level constants remain at module scope
WirePlumber restart recovery — capture signals wired outside ready handler; clear_state() purges stale routing on reconnect
Stream migration after A2DP restore — pactl move-sink-input migrates ALSA-bound streams back to BT sink after calls
Pactl fallback for default sink re-assertion — bypasses stale 3s-polled monitor cache after HSP/A2DP profile switches
BT node cache bypass — bluez_* nodes skip poll data cache since PipeWire recreates them with new IDs after profile switches
Flatpak daemon wrapper — exit code detection prevents restart loop on intentional Ctrl+C shutdown
Bug fixes: activate_bt_card() ordering, handle_capture_stopped re-entrancy guard, read-only profile loader
Desktop notifications, file-based logging, keyboard shortcuts, Import/Export from gear menu
Added shared modules: log.js (structured logging), spawn_sync_with_timeout in utils.js
192 GJS unit tests across 7 test files

description.p: Production-ready release with 51 unit tests, Flatpak support, and capture-aware BT switching.

ul.li: Capture-aware auto-switching between A2DP (music) and HSP/HFP (calls)
Per-card debounce timers and restoring-state gating for reliable BT transitions
Source code refactored: constants, pactl parser, BT profiles, and utils extracted as shared modules
51 GJS unit tests covering config CRUD, daemon routing, and monitor parsing
Flatpak support with org.gnome.Platform//50 (Wp-0.5 typelib optional)
D-Bus session service for UI activation
Heartbeat-based daemon liveness detection in UI
Rename and overwrite protection in profile editor
PipeWire 1.6.6+ compatibility (</> stream arrow detection)
Audio Profile creation, editing, and deletion
Automatic routing via WirePlumber background daemon
systemd user service for automatic startup at login
